CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, string_value VARCHAR(100));
SELECT '<'(string_value, 'ab') AS result FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT '<'(string_value, 'a') AS result FROM test_table WHERE id = 4;
SELECT '<'(string_value, 'abc') AS result FROM test_table WHERE id = 5;
SELECT '<'(s1.string_value, s2.string_value) AS comparison FROM test_table s1, test_table s2 WHERE s1.id != s2.id;
SELECT (SELECT '<'(string_value, 'a') FROM test_table WHERE id = 1) AS compare_a;
SELECT (SELECT '<'(string_value, 'abc') FROM test_table WHERE id = 3) AS compare_abc;
SELECT '<'(string_value, 'a') AS result FROM test_table WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE string_value IS NOT NULL);
SELECT (SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE '<'(string_value, 'ab')) AS matched_ids;
SELECT a.val, t.string_value FROM another_table a JOIN test_table t ON '<'(a.val, t.string_value);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE '<'(string_value, (SELECT MAX(string_value) FROM test_table));
SELECT (CASE WHEN '<'('a', 'b') THEN 'a is less' ELSE 'b is less or equal' END) AS comparison_message;
